title: Shoot texture switching (per_shot mode + while_firing mode) in gun item renderer
reference_files: C:/Users/GGxia/Desktop/YanbweMod/ModularShoot/设计文档.md#L1289-1309 (Gun texture system: texture, shoot_texture, shoot_texture_mode per_shot/while_firing, vanilla pipeline auto-3D)
C:/Users/GGxia/Desktop/YanbweMod/ModularShoot/设计文档.md#L1343-1344 (Render perspective: first person no arm animation, texture switch only; third person vanilla crossbow)
C:/Users/GGxia/Desktop/YanbweMod/ModularShoot/设计文档.md#L1354-1357 (while_firing texture rule: isFiring true -> shoot_texture, false -> base texture)
acceptance_criteria: Gun item renderer integrates with vanilla item rendering pipeline (auto 3D texture processing, no custom model loader)
per_shot mode: on shoot trigger, render texture switches to shoot_texture for one frame, then switches back to base texture (high fire rate = rapid flicker, by design)
while_firing mode: uses isFiring flag from PlayerShootStateManager - isFiring==true -> shoot_texture, isFiring==false -> base texture (no flicker at low fire rate)
No shoot_texture defined -> always uses base texture, shoot_texture_mode has no effect
shoot_texture_mode defaults to 'per_shot' when not specified
Texture switching works in both first-person and third-person views
First-person: no arm animation, texture switch is the only visual feedback
deliverables: src/main/java/org/yanbwe/modularshoot/client/render/GunItemRenderer.java (custom item renderer with texture switching logic)
src/main/java/org/yanbwe/modularshoot/client/render/ShootTextureResolver.java (texture selection logic: per_shot vs while_firing + isFiring check)
